findings.detail: 11 nodes, all exact Python class names, no prose. Edges reproduce the corpus ASCII tree exactly. LookupError-->IndexError and LookupError-->KeyError directly encode why 'except LookupError:' catches both children; OSError-->FileNotFoundError ties to the 7.1 missing-file thread.

PNG re-rendered (56 KB, 1400px) after catch-parent highlight edit, visually inspected. The four intermediate->child edges (ArithmeticError->ZeroDivisionError, LookupError->IndexError, LookupError->KeyError, OSError->FileNotFoundError) are bold orange with 'catch parent' edge labels; a top-left legend box states the rule with the 'except LookupError:' example. Clean 3-level tree, no overlaps, no truncation, all text legible. Tier colors (blue root / purple intermediate / green leaf) intact.

11 nodes exceeds the 3-8 guide, but the intent justifies the full tree: the 'catch parent catches children' and 'order specific-to-general' corpus rules both reference the complete inheritance structure. Not node_overflow.
